description: Instance C (Split planes): an API where the document plane (OpenAPI contract) and the runtime plane (live HTTP server) are forced into exact lockstep. Dominant coherent-and-wrong: blending — a clean schema masking broken runtime behaviour (id leaks, tracebacks, missing auth), or a clean runtime escaping stale documentation.

sources.name: API governance policy (API_POLICY_081)
sources.notes: runtime-plane authority; CLAUSE_1 auth, CLAUSE_2 uuid-only, CLAUSE_3 clean errors

sources.name: Canonical OpenAPI contract
sources.notes: /opt/squeeze/shared/base_schema.json (0444); document-plane authority both bands read independently

sources.name: Document-plane linter (oracle)
sources.notes: ground-truth linter.py; lints the implementer's openapi.json

sources.name: Live server runtime behaviour (oracle)
sources.notes: the running server on 127.0.0.1:8000 — RUN over HTTP, never read as source; the formal 'run, don't read' channel

actors.name: Gate sentinel / no-blend referee
actors.builds: the gate sequence (isolation, coverage, runtime, document plane, ty0) and the loop's SL-1.0 instance-c.sl.json

upper_bound.text: both plane authorities it refuses to let blend

lower_bound.text: linter on the schema, runtime HTTP behaviour, frozen DB, legacy-signature regression, both bands' artifacts

forbidden_moves: edit the server, schema, or tests
let one plane's check stand in for the other

actors.name: API implementer
actors.builds: the HTTP server and its published OpenAPI schema

upper_bound.text: the governance policy and the canonical contract it must satisfy and align to

lower_bound.text: the frozen DB it serves over, and the linter its published schema must pass

forbidden_moves: leak internal ids or tracebacks on the wire

actors.name: Conformance exerciser
actors.builds: the conformance test matrix derived from the policy and the contract

upper_bound.text: the governance policy and the canonical contract, read independently of the implementation

lower_bound.text: what the live server actually returns when run over HTTP (run, never read the source)

forbidden_moves: read the server source or the implementer's openapi.json

disjointness.rationale: The implementer is bound by both plane authorities but reads only the frozen DB and the linter below; the exerciser is bound by the same authorities but its lower bound is the running server observed over HTTP — it RUNS the implementation without ever reading its source. The sentinel certifies only when both planes pass separately and agree, so neither plane's check can mask the other (no-blend).
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: the exerciser runs the server (runtime_oracle) but is physically denied the server source and the implementer's schema (zero-import isolation); the run-vs-read distinction is what keeps the barrier physical

catchability.blind_spot: a blend: a clean schema or happy-path runtime hiding an id leak / missing auth on an untested route
catchability.via: Gate B runtime assertions (status, exact keys, no forbidden patterns) + the document-plane route cross-check

catchability.blind_spot: a weak happy-path matrix that misses a clause corner
catchability.via: Gate C clause coverage + seeded coherent-and-wrong negative controls
